sn38.5:1.1: "Friend Sāriputta, it is said, ‘one who has attained consolation, one who has attained consolation.’ In what way, friend, has one attained consolation?"[n.273] Assasapatta. The answer is a coded formula for the sekha. The next sutta, on paramassāsapatta, concerns the arahant.
sn38.5:1.2: "When, friend, a bhikkhu understands as it really is the origin and the passing away, the gratification, the danger, and the escape in the case of the six bases for contact, in this way he has attained consolation."
sn38.5:1.3: "But, friend, is there a path, is there a way for the realization of this consolation?"
sn38.5:1.4: "There is a path, friend, there is a way … this Noble Eightfold Path … ."
